---
title: "Attention gap case study"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Attention gap case study}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

The attention-gap workflow compares public attention with a conservation-status scale. It is best interpreted as a communication-priority diagnostic.

```{r}
library(conCultuR)
ex <- con_culturomics_example_data()
dict <- score_ambiguity(ex$dictionary)
traces <- collect_attention(dict, platforms = c("google", "wikipedia"), from = "2024-01-01", to = "2024-03-31", mock = TRUE)
idx <- attention_index(standardise_attention(traces), min_platforms = 1)
gap <- attention_gap(idx, ex$conservation_status)
gap_sens <- attention_gap_sensitivity(idx, ex$conservation_status)
head(gap)
head(gap_sens)
```

A strong result should be visible in both the default classification and the sensitivity grid. A concept that appears only under one threshold setting should be described as a weak or near-threshold candidate.
